revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Awai-jinja]][[ja:粟井神社]] {{Expand Japanese|粟井神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Awai Shrine | native_name = 粟井神社 | image = Awaizinzya 11.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Awai Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.091169|133.704517}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|en|Futodama|it|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|ru|Футодама|simple|Futodama|zh|布刀玉命|qid=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Awai Shrine'''|粟井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11632546|ltq=Q11632546|qid=Q11632546|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province}} ** Series ordinal: 21 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041397|ltq=Q135041397|qid=Q135041397|1=Uheno shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kuroshimano shrine|lt=Kuroshimano shrine|qid=Q135041398|1=Kuroshimano shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q861270|ltq=Q861270|qid=Q861270|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} * {{ill|Q929682|ltq=Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} * {{ill|Toyota District (Sanuki Province)|lt=Toyota district|en|Toyota District (Sanuki Province)|ja|豊田郡 (香川県)|zh|豐田郡 (香川縣)|qid=Q44439|1=Toyota District (Sanuki Province)}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.091169|133.704517}} == image (P18) == * Awaizinzya 11.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11059539|ltq=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060723183930/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/556001.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183241 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あわいじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 768-0052https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5470005003946 == Commons category (P373) == * Awai-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '香川県観音寺市粟井町1716番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Awai-jinja}} [[JA:粟井神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11604560}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Awai Shrine |Image = [[File:Awaizinzya_11.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kanonji City|Q861270|qid=Q861270|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} Awai-chō 1716 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Karita Daimyōjin / Hydrangea Shrine / Ajisai Shrine |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 23|Q2958|qid=Q2958|1=October 23}} |Rituals = Hyakute Shinto ritual ({{ill|lt=March 23|Q2445|qid=Q2445|1=March 23}}) |Designation = One of the 24 Shikinaisha of Sanuki Province |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Awai Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Awai Shrine''' (あわいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kanonji City|Q861270|qid=Q861270|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) of Karita District in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} (later {{ill|lt=Toyota District, Kagawa|Q44439|qid=Q44439|1=Toyota District (Sanuki Province)}}, then {{ill|lt=Mitoyo District|Q31312|qid=Q31312|1=Mitoyo District, Kagawa}}), and one of the 24 Shikinaisha of Sanuki Province. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It stands at the foot of Mount Fujime, beside Iwanabe Pond. Approximately 3,000 hydrangea bushes are planted within the shrine grounds, and a Hydrangea Festival is held on the third Sunday of June. The alternate names “Hydrangea Shrine” and “Ajisai Shrine” are said to have originated from these flowers. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === * {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} === Associated deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|1=Ukemochi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} – Enshrined together in the main hall of Suga Shrine. === History === The date of foundation is unknown. According to tradition, when the {{ill|lt=Sanuki Imbe clan|Q11632550|qid=Q11632550|1=Sanuki Inbe clan}} cultivated this land, they enshrined their clan deity, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto. The name “Awai” is said to derive from settlers or a transferred enshrinement from {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} or {{ill|lt=Awa Province (Bōsō)|Q1053044|qid=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}, interpreted as “Aha-i (Awai).” The Awa Imbe Shrine tradition records that during the time of Hisana, the second son of the 31st-generation Takemochi, descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deity was transferred to this location.{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849/1/225 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-15}} Another shrine tradition states: “In the 12th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era (672)… a man named Rokuemon of Kawata Village, Itano District, Awa Province, received a divine oracle… and after constructing a shrine on Toyogaoka, enshrined the three deities {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=Ukemochi}}. In the first year of the Shuchō era (686)… this became the beginning of the clan shrine.” The {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} records that “in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (842), Awai Shrine was elevated to Myōjin status,” suggesting that by the early Heian period it was already a prominent shrine and is therefore presumed to be the oldest shrine in Sanuki. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} notes that on October 15 of the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} (864), it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engi Rinjisai-shiki|1=Engishiki}} lists “Awai Shrine, one seat, Sanuki Province” among the 285 seats of the Myōjin Festival, making it the only shrine in Sanuki Province at the time to participate in this festival. Formerly called Karita Daimyōjin, it is said to have given its name to Karita District (also called Karita or Kanda District). The {{ill|lt=Nihon Kiraku|Q1053542|qid=Q1053542|1=Nihon Kiryaku}} records that in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} (906), Karita Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank. The former site of the shrine is said to have been about 600 meters south of the present location. It burned down in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|qid=Q30016|1=807}}) and was relocated in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|qid=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} ({{ill|lt=1004|Q5049|qid=Q5049|1=1004}}) to the site of Sugio Shrine (now Sugio Shrine within the Awai Shrine grounds), which is the current location. During the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, {{ill|lt=Fujime Castle|Q17212005|qid=Q17212005|1=Fujime Castle}} stood atop Mount Fujime. In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|qid=Q6707|1=1578}}), {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|qid=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}} persuaded and subdued Saitō Shimōsa-no-Kami, lord of Fujime Castle. The Ōnishi clan of Awa allied with the Chōsokabe, and through this connection the Saitō clan became their vassals. Motochika stationed Kuwana Tarōzaemon and Hamada Zen’emon at Fujime Castle, but Miyoshi Moriyasu (Sogō Moriyasu) of Awa ordered his retainer Nara Tarōbei Katsumasa, lord of Shōtsūji Castle, to attack Fujime Castle. Along with Nagao Ōsumi-no-Kami, Hatoko Izu-no-Kami, and Kagawa Minbu-no-Shō, the Nara clan captured Fujime Castle. The Chōsokabe forces, having lost the castle, later captured Motozasa Castle in Zaita, Mino District, and used it as an outpost to retake Fujime Castle in winter. This became the prelude to Motochika’s conquest of Sanuki. Although it is often said that the wars in Sanuki began with Chōsokabe Motochika, in reality they began when the Miyoshi attacked Fujime Castle, which had been under Chōsokabe control. It is also said that Awai Shrine burned down in these conflicts, losing its buildings, records, and treasures, but no historical evidence confirms this, and it remains unclear which faction was responsible. As with many shrines and temples in Kagawa Prefecture, traditions persist that they were burned in warfare without clarity as to which faction was responsible, and such destruction is often attributed to Chōsokabe Motochika. Awai Shrine is no exception and is likewise said to have been burned by the Chōsokabe. Although the Saitō clan fought on the Chōsokabe side and, after defeat by the Miyoshi forces, fled to Awa, shrine tradition states that it was the Chōsokabe who attacked the Saitō clan. Later, the site of the second bailey of Fujime Castle was designated as an otabisho (temporary resting place for deities) and was used until the early Shōwa period (now Fujime Shrine within the Awai Shrine grounds). In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|qid=Q635245|1=Genna}} ({{ill|lt=1620|Q6810|qid=Q6810|1=1620}}), it was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Ikoma Masatoshi|Q11574929|qid=Q11574929|1=Ikoma Masatoshi}}. In the 12th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}}), it was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. In the 13th year of {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} ({{ill|lt=2001|Q1988|qid=Q1988|1=2001}}), the current shrine buildings were constructed. === Shrine grounds === Awaizinzya 0ɾ.jpg|Main hall Awaizinzya 02.jpg|Kagura hall Awaizinzya 17.jpg|Hydrangeas Awaizinzya 15.jpg|Hydrangeas === Auxiliary shrines === [[File:Awaizinzya 0ɜ.jpg|180px|thumb|Sugio Shrine]] From the rear left of the main hall toward the front: * Sugio Shrine (small shrine): Enshrined slightly higher than the main hall, as the deity was originally worshipped at this site. * Aramitama Shrine (small shrine): Relocated from the Tsunetsugu area. * Awashima Shrine (small shrine): Relocated from Suga Shrine on Mount Kamiyama. * Chinomi Shrine: A long stone rather than a shrine building, relocated from the Takenari area. * Itsukushima Shrine (small shrine) [[File:Awaizinzya 05.jpg|180px|thumb|Group of auxiliary shrines]] On the rear right of the main hall: * Inari Shrine * Ryūō Shrine * Yorei Shrine * Hiraki Shrine * Fujime Shrine * Tenmangū Shrine === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Kanonji Station (Kagawa)|Q5358548|qid=Q5358548|1=Kan'onji Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|qid=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} of {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|Q496531|qid=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === Events === [[File:Awaizinzya 18.jpg|thumb|250px|Shrine grounds during the Hydrangea Festival]] * Hydrangea Festival: From 10:00 a.m. to 1:30 p.m., featuring Taishō-goto performances, marching band parades, Awai Ondo, poetry recitation, belly dancing, Japanese taiko drumming, and goldfish scooping. Udon and rice balls were sold, and shuttle buses operated from below the shrine. (2019-06-16) {{-}} === Surroundings === {{Right| Awaizinzya 0ɵ.jpg|Summit plateau of Mount Fujime Awaizinzya 19.jpg|Iwanabe Pond }} ;Around Mount Fujime * {{ill|lt=Fujime Castle|Q17212005|qid=Q17212005|1=Fujime Castle}} ruins: Elevation 136.5 m. Said to have been the residence of the Saitō clan in the early Muromachi period before falling in the 6th year of Tenshō. A replica of the Shikoku 88-temple pilgrimage was created on the summit plateau in the 3rd year of Taishō to commemorate the 1100th memorial of Kōbō Daishi.From an informational sign installed in Heisei 26 beside Fudō-in About 30 m uphill from Fudō-in. * Fujime Inari: Shōichii Fujime Inari Daimyōjin. Relocated from the Deharu area. About 10 m uphill from Fudō-in. * Tsurugi-yama Shrine: Tsurugi-san Gongen. Relocated from Ikenouchi in the Okutani area. Located behind Fudō-in. * Fujimeyama Fudō-in: Enshrines Fudō Myōō and Senju Kannon. Accessible by car up to this point. ; Awai Dam * Part of the Awai River in the Saita River system; a gravity-type concrete dam with a total storage capacity of 590,000 m³, completed in 2002.From Dam Card DATA === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Kagawa Prefecture Association of Shinto Priests (ed.), *Shrines of Kagawa Prefecture, Vol. 2*, Kagawa Prefecture Association of Shinto Priests, 1938. * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), *Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 23: Nankaidō*, Kōgakkan University Press, 1987. * Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), *Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 2: Sanyō and Shikoku*, Hakusuisha, 1984. * “Awai Shrine,” Institute for Shinto Studies, Association of Shinto Shrines (ed.), *Heisei ‘Matsuri’ Data (CD-ROM)*, National Survey Committee on Shrine Rites and Festivals, 1995. === External links === * [https://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/21/13861.html Awai Shrine – The Hydrangea Shrine] – {{ill|lt=Kanonji City|Q861270|qid=Q861270|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Awai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Awai Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kannonji]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kan'onji, Kagawa]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]]
